title: Odd or even

content.problemStatement: Write a program that allows the user to insert integer number, then check If a number is even or odd
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "Even" if N is even, otherwise print "Odd". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 8 is divisible by 2, so the program prints "Even".
1 leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 2, so the program prints "Odd".
10 is even and 11 is odd, producing the two lines accordingly.

solution.approach: The parity of an integer can be determined by checking the least significant bit or by computing N % 2. If the remainder is 0 the number is even, otherwise it is odd. We read the number of test cases T, then process each N independently, outputting the appropriate word. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides the input and output buffers.
